wrote: (binding [ctx store, tx t] ...)
expected: comma = whitespace (faithful Clojure)
reality: beagle read , as unquote (CL-style), ~ unwired; silent misparse

reality: beagle/clj rejected; clojure.core dynamics not seeded as dyn vars
fix: #24 check.rkt:676

wrote: rt/re-* extern workaround for regex literals
expected: (inherited secondhand) regex literals are a reader gap
reality: regex literals work natively; the gap never existed; workaround built on an unverified inherited claim

wrote: ~x as unquote on nix

reality: nix reader squats ~ for tilde-strings; ~=unquote fragmented by target
fix: #25 remove tilde-string sugar, ~=unquote uniform

wrote: #lang beagle/clj on a flip-view module
expected: #lang round-trips through the graph as #lang
reality: read-beagle-syntax mutates #lang to (define-target clj) node; render emits (define-target); beagle-build rejects it

wrote: (if-let [[_ a b] expr] ...) and (when-let [g :- T expr] ...)
expected: Clojure destructuring + :- in if-let/when-let
reality: beagle rejects; bindings must be [name expr]

expected: lambda :- (U ...) return is rejected (inherited claim)
reality: already works; beagle-1 probed, not a gap; false report (probe-don't-inherit)
fix: no change (#22 probe)

wrote: (def ^:dynamic *x* nil) + (binding [*x* 1] ...)
expected: Clojure dynamic vars
reality: ^:dynamic hard-rejected (no ^-meta reader); earmuff def emitted without ^:dynamic then runtime-throws (silent miscompile)

wrote: (str/split s re 3)
expected: Clojure str/split 3-arg limit
reality: beagle stdlib-clj had no 3-arg str/split

wrote: (java.io.FileOutputStream. path true)
expected: FQCN constructor recognized as a new-form + typed
reality: constructor-sym? required uppercase FIRST char, so FQCN ctors (lowercase package) misparsed as undefined calls -> Any; receiver-typing silently bypassed

wrote: (Arr String) / (Arr KeyManager) unsized array annotations
expected: spec step 7 implied unsized (Arr T) needed adding to parse-type
reality: already parses + type-checks (one-arg type-app); only manifest element-precision was missing

wrote: lambda (fn [x] :- (U Int String) ...)
expected: union return on lambda might be a gap (spec #22 listed it)
reality: already works; probed before building
fix: none-needed

target: gjoa GjoaLoader.bjs runtime binding + chrome bundler (loadSubScript .uc.js path)
wrote: a loader loop binding every core.js export as window $$bc$<exportName>, assuming the named-import LOCAL suffix equals the EXPORT name
expected: $$bc$equiv binds export equiv (suffix==export)
reality: $$bc$equiv imports export equivV not equiv — the lite/full SIZE-leg split keeps the local name stable while the backing export varies, so the local-to-export mapping is NOT derivable from the name; it must be read from the beagle import line itself
fix: bundler rewrites the stripped import of equivV-as-$$bc$equiv from beagle/core.js into a const $$bc$equiv = $$bc.equivV prelude, reading the exact export-to-local mapping from the import clause; no naming-convention guessing

reality: check PASSES but emit mis-parses the bracket fn-type return as a 2nd-arity param vector -> broken multi-arity ([src] :-)([String -> Any] body). check/emit divergence.
fix: :- Any at the closure-return boundary; gap-listed (beagle should emit bracket fn-type returns, or reject them at check not emit)
wrote: (defn make-xresolve [src :- String] :- [String -> Any] body)
expected: 1-arity fn whose RETURN type is a fn-type [String -> Any]

reality: bb/SCI resolves symbols top-to-bottom at analysis -> 'Unable to resolve symbol: walk!' (walk-all! calls walk! defined later). JVM Clojure interns at def-time so wouldn't fail.
fix: add (declare ...) for the mutual-recursion cluster in source so emit includes it; beagle clj-emit could auto-emit declares for forward-referenced defns (bb-robustness)
wrote: mutually-recursive defns with NO (declare) (beagle check resolves forward refs fine)
expected: emitted clj loads on bb

reality: bb/SCI qualified access ns/name needs foo INTERNED in ns; :refer only enables BARE foo within the ns -> resolve/resolve-warm-store! unresolved
fix: make the Beagle module ns = resolve (interns all defs + dynvars) instead of a chartroom.resolve + :refer shim
wrote: (ns resolve (:require [chartroom.resolve :refer :all])) to re-export so consumers' resolve/foo works
expected: :refer :all makes resolve/foo + resolve/<dynvar> resolvable from another ns (qualified)

wrote: (s "hello " name) direct interpolation form
expected: #25(a) said (s …)/(ms …) forms must be BUILT
reality: already exist — the tilde reader desugars ~"…" -> (s …) and ~''…'' -> (ms …); parser+emit handle them; (s …) builds byte-identical to the tilde form
fix: probed; migration is AST-preserving -> drvPath-eq automatic

title: cross-module binding of a required module's ^:dynamic var is rejected
detail: (binding [a/*v* val] ...) where a/*v* is (def ^:dynamic *v* ...) in a REQUIRED module errors: 'a/*v* is not a dynamic var — only (def ^:dynamic a/*v* ...) vars can be rebound with binding'. Diverges from Clojure (which binds any other-ns dynamic var). Same-module binding + defmacro both work. Blocks porting the daemon's resolver-woven handlers (bind 15+ resolve/* dynvars).
workaround: thunk-binding fn in the OWNING module (same-module binding is legal; the caller's thunk runs in the dynamic scope at runtime — proven: cross-module thunk reads bound value).
probe: fram.cdgap-a/b (cross-module binding rejected; thunk variant returns BOUND)

title: G-A cross-module ^:dynamic binding only works SAME-DIRECTORY
detail: (binding [r/ctx ..]) of a required module's ^:dynamic var type-checks ONLY when the requiring module is in the SAME DIR as the dynvar's defining module. Probe pbind.bclj binding r/ctx: 0 errors in chartroom/src (next to resolve.bclj); 'r/ctx is not a dynamic var' in src/fram. Cross-dir the require resolves fns (r/ord-pos? works) but the imported-dynamic-vars set (G-A 8083fcb) is not populated. Also: a defmacro that syntax-quotes the binding qualifies r/ctx -> resolve/ctx, which the use-site-keyed import set also misses (so use a thunk-fn, not a macro).
workaround: relocate the binding module to the dynvar module's directory (coord_resolver.bclj -> chartroom/src); use a thunk-fn not a macro.

resolves: cross-module binding of a required module's ^:dynamic var is rejected
title: G-A cross-module ^:dynamic binding now type-checks (CLOSED)
detail: Fixed at root (8083fcb): check.rkt seeds the importer with each imported ^:dynamic var's dynamic-ness keyed by use-site name; parse.rkt/ast.rkt carry it. (binding [a/*v* val] ...) over a required module's dynvar checks clean, matching Clojure. Precision preserved: binding a NON-dynamic imported var still errors 'not a dynamic var'.
verified_by: beagle-test/tests/cross-module-dynvar.rkt EXIT=0 @3271c40 — ok.bclj (pos) + bad.bclj (neg precision guard, check-exn), non-vacuous

resolves: G-A cross-module ^:dynamic binding only works SAME-DIRECTORY
title: G-A cross-DIR + via-MACRO ^:dynamic binding now type-checks (CLOSED)
detail: Cross-directory require now populates the imported-dynamic-vars set (resolved via module-path walk-up); the macro-expanded shape keeps the use-site alias p/*v* (not prov/*v*) so the use-site-keyed import set still resolves dynamic-ness post-expansion. fram-2's exact repro (cross-dir AND via a syntax-quoting macro, resolver-woven handler shape) checks clean — the workaround (relocate module / thunk-not-macro) is no longer needed. Unblocks the resolver-woven daemon port.

wrote: (theme :colors (background "oklch(...)" ...)) in fleet.eddy expecting :root CSS vars to be emitted
expected: theme form lowers to a :root custom-property style block so text-foreground/bg-background utility classes resolve
reality: reader parsed (theme ...) into IrTheme and graph threaded prog.theme, but codegen.bjs had ZERO theme handling — silently dropped; styling only worked because dist.css ALSO hardcodes the same :root vars (form was a redundant no-op)
fix: eddy codegen.bjs: added gen-theme-style, wired into gen-view-program! init

wrote: clicked an agent row expecting window.eddy.selection.get() to return the uuid for the Cytoscape/stream mount-points
expected: the view's selectItem() pushes selection (identity) onto the shared window.eddy.selection bus so custom panes highlight in lockstep
reality: feed mode installs eddy.selection {set/get} but codegen's selectItem(eid) only set internal selectedEid + rebuilt the detail panel — never called eddy.selection.set; seam was half-wired (feed->pane worked, selection->pane did not); get() returned null after a real click
fix: eddy codegen.bjs: added _eddyPushSelection(eid->identity) + guarded call in selectItem

wrote: bin/eddy-compile demo/fleet.eddy out.js per README (root-level bin/)
expected: eddy-compile lives at ~/code/eddy/bin/ as the README/claude.md show
reality: compile entry is actually ~/code/eddy/web/bin/eddy-compile; no bin/ at repo root; README path is stale
fix: used web/bin/eddy-compile; README path drift, not a real gap

wrote: beagle build <verb-authored .fram/code.log triples> --build-edn
expected: the fram code log's slot edges are plain fN, so --build-edn rebuilds every form
reality: verb-authored (upsert/insert) forms are wired with #36 CRDT positional slots f<bignum>~<tie> (e.g. f262144~12); beagle ref-pred?/ordered-fN only match ^f[0-9]+$, so it silently drops every CRDT-positioned form and emits only the seed's plain f0/f1/f2
fix: compile via fram-render-code (resolve.clj normalizes CRDT slots to plain fN) -> beagle build TEXT; root fix = teach claims-roundtrip.rkt ref-pred?/ordered-fN the f<path>~<tie> spelling + fractional sort

wrote: beagle build <daemon :render EDN projection> --build-edn
expected: the daemon-normalized :render EDN (plain fN) builds straight through
reality: resolve.clj's projected EDN encodes the :- type marker such that beagle's read-edn-triples reconstructs it as '#:- ' (namespaced-map reader artifact), so def parse fails: 'malformed def ... got (def salutation #:- String "Howdy")'
fix: use fram-render-code TEXT path instead of feeding :render EDN to --build-edn; root fix = align resolve.clj leaf encoding for keyword v with claims-roundtrip encode-leaf/decode-leaf

wrote: --build-edn over a fram code-log with verb-authored forms
expected: all forms (seed + CRDT-positioned) survive the claims->syntax build
reality: claims-roundtrip ref/order only matched ^f[0-9]+$ (legacy slot); fram verbs position children with #36 CRDT keys f<path>~<tie> (e.g. f262144~12), so ordered-fN's sequential f0/f1 loop stopped at the first gap and SILENTLY DROPPED every verb-authored form (kept seed only)
fix: claims-roundtrip.rkt parse-fN-slot/ordered-slot-children + ref-pred? — dual fN/f<path>~<tie> parse, sort by (path,tie); mirrors fram resolve.bclj ord-parse/ord-cmp

wrote: author (def x :- T v) via fram-code-author inline :form EDN
expected: :- stored as a beagle symbol node (kind symbol, v ":-") like the reader produces
reality: bb's EDN/Clojure reader reads beagle's :- type-marker as a KEYWORD (kind keyword, v "-"); --build-edn then decodes it to Racket #:- and the parser rejects (def x #:- T v). Oracle text-bridge launders it (renders keyword named - back to :- text). fram-author-side bug, NOT beagle roundtrip; out of scope for this beagle fix
fix: fram-lease fram-code-author must read :form with beagle's reader, or normalize :- keyword->symbol; logged for fram side

wrote: M2 tuple placeholder: if/cond/then/else and record/field as distinct node kinds/predicates
expected: conditionals and records need bespoke claim shapes (if-node with then/else edges; record-node with field/N)
reality: NO bespoke shapes — `if` and `defrecord` are plain `list` nodes headed by the symbol `if`/`defrecord`; the field vector is the same `#%brackets` list as a defn param vector. Uniform list+fN.
fix: G0 swap: PROMPTSET E4/E5 placeholders replaced with engine-dumped list+fN tuples

wrote: M2 tuple placeholder: (:x p) field-access as access/of/field; cross-def call via a `callee` predicate
expected: field-access and cross-definition calls need dedicated predicates (access/of/field, callee @id)
reality: NO dedicated predicates — `(:x p)` is a plain `list` headed by the keyword-accessor leaf `:x` (encoded kind=symbol, v=":x", colon retained like `:-`); a cross-def call's callee is just a symbol leaf bound by resolution via bound_to. No `callee` edge.
fix: G0 swap: PROMPTSET E9/E10 placeholders replaced with engine-dumped tuples

wrote: M2 edit placeholder: change a defn return type by superseding a single claim [@id ret Nat]
expected: a return type / signature field is a directly-supersepable claim (a `ret` predicate on the defn node)
reality: FANTASY — there is no `ret`/signature predicate; a return type is an ordinary fN child SLOT of the form list, changeable only by re-minting the whole form (upsert-replace ~40 ops) or a body edit. No surgical signature verb. (Also `Nat` is not a valid beagle type.)
fix: G0: E8 substituted to a set-body edit (the engine's genuine surgical edit); gap noted in FREEZE.md form-support boundary

wrote: relied on fram-code-author / fram-edit-code :edit-min to recompile-gate the M2 changeset fail-closed
expected: the warm authoring tool the brief names runs the beagle typecheck/recompile gate before committing (per the canonical prompt's 'recompile-gated, fail-closed')
reality: the :edit-min warm path runs ONLY the structural verb checks (collision, orphaned-ref); it does NOT typecheck — a return-Nat defn LANDED unchecked. The recompile gate (beagle-build-all '0 error') lives in a DIFFERENT caller: fram_mcp.clj route-edit/flip-graph-edit!. M2 must be gated through that path or it gets a free pass M1 doesn't (C1/G3 confound).
fix: G0/D3 finding: harness MUST route M2 through the recompile gate (render->beagle-build-all), not bare :edit-min; recorded in FREEZE.md D3

wrote: assumed beagle-build-all --build-edn could ingest the model's @m#N / keyword-pred EDN triples directly
reality: --build-edn (build-all.rkt -> edn-triples->syntax) requires the ENGINE dump shape: INTEGER node ids, STRING predicates ("kind"/"v"/"fN"), bare-int objects for fN/child/tail refs, and a leading @file header; the model's @m#N strings + keyword preds are a HARNESS surface that must be translated. Built m2_adapter.bb to map @m#N/keyword -> integer/string dump (renumber above current-max, resolve @-refs from state name-map). No fram-engine change (R3 'harness adapter maps EDN->fram claims').
fix: experiments/authoring-bakeoff/harness/m2_adapter.bb (verified: E1/E2/E4/E5/E7 round-trip + build 0 error)

wrote: R3 directive illustrated M2 triples as ["@m#1" :head "defn"] — a :head predicate carrying the operator string
reality: the engine has NO :head predicate; a list's head is :f0 -> a symbol leaf (kind=symbol, v="defn"). :head is not engine-faithful to list/fN/leaf. Read R3's binding constraint ('engine-faithful to list/fN/leaf') as authoritative over the illustrative :head spelling; rendered all examples in the explicit :f0->leaf form (consistency between examples+contract is load-bearing — the model imitates examples). Adapter ALSO desugars a stray :head for robustness, but no example teaches it.
fix: FREEZE.md ':head shorthand — judgment call' note; gen-prompts.bb renders :f0->leaf

wrote: graded the emitted clj by referencing beagle.user/fn as a compile-time symbol after (load-file ...) in the same babashka script
reality: SCI (babashka) resolves fully-qualified symbols at ANALYSIS time, BEFORE load-file runs -> 'Unable to resolve symbol: beagle.user/square' even though the fn is defined. NOT a beagle surface issue — a bb/SCI eval-order gotcha. Fix: grade via (eval (read-string "<test>")) at RUNTIME after the ns loads.
fix: harness/run.bb grade fn: runtime (eval (read-string ...)) instead of analysis-time symbol ref

wrote: expected (persist :feed URL) + (view … :select detail :add-form …) to let a feed-backed entity WRITE an edit back (POST) declaratively, the way :server persistence syncs add/update/delete
reality: eddy's :feed persist mode is READ-ONLY by design (reader.bjs: 'store mirrors an external HTTP endpoint that returns a JSON array; poll + store.load(). Read-only.'). There is no declarative write-back form for a feed store; :add-form on a feed view only adds to the LOCAL mirror, never POSTs. The sanctioned seam for mutation is the (panel … :mount fn) escape hatch, which receives { el, eddy } and shares window.eddy.{selection,feed,stores} — so the edit form is shell-owned JS that POSTs and lets the feed re-render. Worked AROUND via that documented panel mount (same pattern fleet.eddy uses for the Cytoscape pane). Candidate eddy feature: a :writable feed or a declarative (edit-form …) that emits the POST + optimistic re-render, so 'edit the graph' is fully declarative.
fix: framescope/web/schema-editor.html schemaEditor.mountEditor pane + bridge POST /schema; gap noted for an eddy :writable-feed / declarative edit-form

wrote: assumed eddy's (persist :feed URL) could mirror framescope's /graph endpoint directly the way fleet.eddy mirrors /presence
reality: eddy's feed loader (codegen.bjs loadFromFeed -> applyFeedRows) fetches the URL and expects a FLAT JSON ARRAY keyed by the entity's :unique identity field; /graph returns {nodes,edges} with NESTED attrs and lifecycle that is DERIVED client-side (abandoned/outcome/committed attrs + driver/depends_on edges). /presence works in fleet.eddy because the bridge already flattens it server-side. No bridge route may be added here, so the graph->rows projection + lifecycle derivation lives in the host-shell as a fetch shim on the feed URL — the same mount-point seam fleet.eddy uses, on the client side of the wire.
fix: framescope/web/board-eddy.html fetch-shim on /board-threads -> projectThreads()

wrote: expected a grouped/kanban eddy view over lifecycle (6 columns) plus drag-between-columns to be expressible in the (view ...) surface
reality: eddy (view :entity :each :add-form :title :select) is a SINGLE list-with-detail; it has no multi-column/grouped layout and no drag-and-drop primitive. The :filters / :date-filters options exist but are SERVER-MODE ONLY (codegen.bjs gates filter-fields-meta behind is-server), so they don't render in a :feed-backed frontend app. The kanban columns + drag->POST /edge claim-write therefore live in a host-shell (panel ... :mount) pane reading window.eddy.stores.threads + eddy.selection — eddy expressed the entity, the card, the :select detail drawer, and the live feed binding cleanly; the kanban+DnD is the escape hatch.
fix: framescope/web/board.eddy (panel board-kanban :mount framescope.mountKanban) + board-eddy.html mountKanban
